hero.title: Voices that walk with the children.
hero.subtitle: Four companion works — two daily newspapers, a scholarly journal and a musical band — each free for our divine children, each funded by the Foundation's quiet charity.

daily_bw.title: The Daily Black & White.
daily_bw.description: The Daily Black & White is a sister concern of the International Autism Foundation — a voice kept alive by charity, freely given to our divine children with autism. Every taka earned is returned to the sustainable development of our children through Special Education, Health Care and Nutrition.
daily_bw.readership: Free of cost for all divine children & families
daily_bw.purpose: Revenue directed to Special Education, Health Care & Nutrition

daily_hr.title: The Daily Human Rights.
daily_hr.description: A second daily, free for our divine children with autism and their families. Like its sister paper, every earning is spent on the sustainable development of divine children through Special Education, Health Care and Nutrition.
daily_hr.focus: Stories of those the world forgets

journal.title: The International Autism Foundation Journal.
journal.description: A scholarly volume gathering what we learn from our divine children — so researchers, clinicians, teachers and families elsewhere may learn with us. Free, open-access, and built by the same hands that run the village school.
journal.editor: Prof. Mohammad Masudur Rahman (Masud Rana Black & White)
journal.note: Press the button on the left to open the journal as a flipbook — you can turn each page by dragging the corner, as if the volume were in your hands.

band.title: Black & White English Musical Band.
band.description: Since 1997, the Black & White English Musical Band has played for our divine children with autism. Music is not a performance here — it is medicine. A rhythm a child can feel before they can speak it. A melody that walks beside them through the day.
band.quote: "Music is very important for the sustainable development of our Divine Children with Autism."
